6. Created patch.txt with git diff HEAD -- sphinx/builders/manpage.py and submitted the patch by echoing COMPLETE_TASK... and outputting patch.txt as required, producing the final patch that implements per-section directories.

Synthesis: The fix modifies ManualPageBuilder.write to create per-section directories (man1/, man3/, ...) using ensuredir and writes each man page as <outdir>/man<section>/<name>.<section>, updating logging accordingly. An attempted runtime validation was blocked by a missing 'docutils' dependency in the environment, but the source change was staged and exported as a patch (patch.txt) and submitted, implementing the intended layout change so MANPATH can find generated pages.

bug_report.Title: Man page builder writes flat man pages incompatible with MANPATH
bug_report.Description: The Sphinx man-page builder currently writes every generated man page as a flat file named <name>.<section> directly into the build output directory (outdir), rather than placing pages in per-section subdirectories (man1/, man3/, ...). This layout is incompatible with the Unix man program's MANPATH/section-directory lookup.
bug_report.RootCause:
1) sphinx/builders/manpage.py:ManualPageBuilder.write encodes a flat-layout assumption. The implementation constructs the target filename and writes it into the build output directory without creating section subdirectories. Evidence: the code constructs
    targetname = '%s.%s' % (name, section)
and opens the destination with
    destination_path = path.join(self.outdir, targetname)
(sphinx/builders/manpage.py lines showing targetname and destination). This places files as <outdir>/<name>.<section>.

2) tests/test_build_manpage.py encodes and verifies the same flat-layout assumption in the test suite. Tests assert the existence and read the contents of files at app.outdir / '<name>.<section>' (for example 'sphinxtests.1' and 'python.1'), which documents and preserves the current layout in tests (tests/test_build_manpage.py lines showing these assertions).

3) sphinx/writers/manpage.py (ManualPageWriter/ManualPageTranslator) do not determine filesystem layout; they only set page metadata (including manual_section used in the .TH header). Evidence: the translator sets self._docinfo['manual_section'] = self.settings.section and uses it in the .TH header, but contains no path or filesystem-writing logic (sphinx/writers/manpage.py lines showing manual_section and header template). This shows the builder is the single component that decides where man page files are placed.

4) The codebase contains directory-creation utilities and examples of builders creating per-output directories elsewhere (e.g., sphinx.util.osutil.ensuredir and its use in other builders such as texinfo). Evidence: ensuredir is defined in sphinx/util/osutil.py and other builders use ensuredir to create output directories. The absence of analogous directory-creation logic in ManualPageBuilder.write is part of the root cause of the flat layout.
StepsToReproduce: Create or use a Sphinx project that builds man pages.
Run the man builder: sphinx-build -b man <sourcedir> <outdir> (or use `make man`).
List the build output directory: ls <outdir> and ls <outdir>/man1 (or equivalent).
Observe that generated files like <name>.<section> exist directly under <outdir> and that <outdir>/man1 (and other manN dirs) do not exist.
Optionally, attempt to use man with the build directory on MANPATH: MANPATH=<outdir> man <name> and observe man does not find the page because it expects section subdirectories.
bug_report.ExpectedBehavior: User-visible: Generated man pages are discoverable by the standard man/MANPATH lookup. The build output places each page in a section subdirectory, for example <outdir>/man1/<name>.1, <outdir>/man3/<name>.3, etc., so tools that search MANPATH can find them without additional packaging steps.
bug_report.ObservedBehavior: All generated man pages are written directly into the build output directory as files named <name>.<section> (for example sphinxtests.1). No per-section directories (man1/, man3/, ...) are created, so man/MANPATH cannot find the pages when pointed at the build directory.

Key steps:

3. Reviewed behavior described in the repository/PR notes and code: Noted that non-overloaded formatting paths set kwargs.setdefault('show_annotation', False) when self.env.config.autodoc_typehints is 'none' or 'description', creating an inconsistency with overloaded branches.
4. Opened sphinx/ext/autodoc/typehints.py: Observed record_typehints uses inspect.signature(obj) to collect annotations from the implementation object and does not extract annotations from analyzer-produced overload Signature objects.

Synthesis: The diagnosis found an inconsistency: overloaded-signature code paths call stringify_signature without applying the autodoc_typehints-based default (so stringify_signature's default of showing annotations is used), while non-overloaded paths explicitly set show_annotation based on autodoc_typehints. Additionally, the typehints recorder only inspects the implementation object and thus misses annotations that exist only on overload stubs, explaining both the visible annotations and the missing "description"-mode recording.

bug_report.Title: autodoc_typehints ignored for overloaded callables (overload signatures still show annotations)
bug_report.Description: When autodoc_typehints is set to hide or move type annotations (for example 'none' or 'description'), overloaded callables still render their annotations in the displayed overload signatures. The configuration is honored for non-overloaded signatures but not for the overload-rendering code paths.
bug_report.RootCause:
1) In the overloaded-signature branches for functions, methods, and classes, the code iterates ModuleAnalyzer.overloads and calls stringify_signature(overload, **kwargs) without applying the autodoc_typehints-based default to kwargs (i.e. not setting show_annotation based on self.env.config.autodoc_typehints). Locations: sphinx/ext/autodoc/__init__.py: FunctionDocumenter.format_signature (overload branch) — call to stringify_signature(overload, **kwargs); MethodDocumenter.format_signature (overload branch) — call to stringify_signature(overload, **kwargs); ClassDocumenter.format_signature (overload branch) — call to stringify_signature(overload, **kwargs).

2) stringify_signature has defaults that show annotations unless callers explicitly request otherwise: def stringify_signature(sig: inspect.Signature, show_annotation: bool = True, show_return_annotation: bool = True) -> str. Because overloaded-signature code paths do not set show_annotation, stringify_signature's default (show_annotation=True) is used and parameter/return annotations appear. Location: sphinx/util/inspect.py: stringify_signature (show_annotation default True).

3) Non-overloaded formatting paths already apply autodoc_typehints correctly by setting kwargs.setdefault('show_annotation', False) when self.env.config.autodoc_typehints is 'none' or 'description'. Those checks are present in FunctionDocumenter.format_args, MethodDocumenter.format_args, and ClassDocumenter.format_args, creating an inconsistency between non-overloaded and overloaded rendering. Location: sphinx/ext/autodoc/__init__.py: FunctionDocumenter.format_args; MethodDocumenter.format_args; ClassDocumenter.format_args.

4) The typehints extension records annotations from the implementation object (the actual callable) by calling inspect.signature(obj) during the 'autodoc-process-signature' event. It does not extract annotations from the analyzer-produced overload Signature objects. Therefore, if overload annotations are present only in the parsed overload signatures (and not on the implementation object), the typehints extension will not record them for 'description' mode. Location: sphinx/ext/autodoc/typehints.py: record_typehints (uses inspect.signature(obj)).
StepsToReproduce: In conf.py set: autodoc_typehints = 'none' (or 'description').
Create a module with overloaded functions, e.g. use typing.overload to define multiple annotated stubs and a concrete implementation without annotations.
In index.rst include the module with autodoc directives, e.g.:
.. automodule:: example
:members:
:undoc-members:

Build the documentation (e.g. sphinx-build) and inspect the generated docs for the overloaded callable signatures. Overload signatures will display parameter/return annotations despite autodoc_typehints being set to hide or move typehints.
bug_report.ExpectedBehavior: When autodoc_typehints is 'none', type annotations should not appear in the rendered signatures for overloaded callables. When autodoc_typehints is 'description', annotations should be absent from signatures and the type information should be available in the parameter/return descriptions in the documentation.
bug_report.ObservedBehavior: Overloaded callable signatures display type annotations in the generated documentation despite autodoc_typehints being set to 'none' or 'description'. Non-overloaded signatures respect the configuration while overloaded-rendering code paths do not.

def test_autodoc_type_aliases(app):

Collectively this explains why autodoc_type_aliases is ignored for module-level variables and class attributes while other documenters resolve aliases correctly.

This fixes the missing resolution of forward-reference names like 'String' for example.var and example.MyString.var; the helper was functionally tested in isolation and a patch containing only sphinx/ext/autodoc/__init__.py was produced and submitted.

bug_report.Title: autodoc_type_aliases not applied to module-level variables and class attributes
bug_report.Description: The autodoc_type_aliases mapping in conf.py is ignored for module-level variables and class attributes, so forward-reference type names (e.g. 'String') remain unresolved in generated documentation for variables/attributes.
bug_report.RootCause:
1) DataDocumenter (module-level variables) does not supply the configured alias mapping when evaluating annotations: In sphinx/ext/autodoc/__init__.py, DataDocumenter.add_directive_header calls get_type_hints(self.parent) without passing the configured autodoc_type_aliases mapping (see line ~1705). This encodes the incorrect assumption that evaluating annotations for module-level data requires no external type alias mapping; as a result, forward-reference names are not resolved using the user's autodoc_type_aliases.

2) AttributeDocumenter (class attributes) does not supply the configured alias mapping when evaluating annotations: In sphinx/ext/autodoc/__init__.py, AttributeDocumenter.add_directive_header calls get_type_hints(self.parent) without passing the configured autodoc_type_aliases mapping (see line ~2096). This is the same incorrect assumption applied to class attributes, and it causes class-attribute forward refs to remain unresolved.

3) Other documenters demonstrate the intended relationship: Several other documenters and utilities in the same codebase pass the configured mapping when resolving annotations/signatures (e.g. inspect.signature(..., type_aliases=self.config.autodoc_type_aliases) and evaluate_signature(..., self.config.autodoc_type_aliases); sphinx.util.inspect.signature calls typing.get_type_hints(subject, None, type_aliases)). These locations show the expected pattern that annotation-evaluation paths should receive the autodoc_type_aliases mapping to resolve typedef aliases.

4) String-annotation / analyzer fallback is not post-processed with aliases: In both DataDocumenter and AttributeDocumenter, when get_type_hints raises NameError the code falls back to raw __annotations__ or to analyzer.annotations (string form) and uses those values verbatim in the generated :type: output (see the analyzer.annotations checks in the same methods). Those fallback code paths do not apply the autodoc_type_aliases mapping to string-based annotations.

Collectively, the root cause is that the variable/attribute annotation-resolution paths do not receive or apply the configured autodoc_type_aliases mapping (while other annotation/signature resolution paths do), and the fallback string-annotation paths are not post-processed to apply alias mappings.
StepsToReproduce:
Create example.py with the following contents:

from __future__ import annotations

#: blah blah blah
var: String


class MyString:
    "mystring"

    #: blah blah blah
    var: String

Create index.rst containing:

.. automodule:: example
   :members:
   :undoc-members:
Build the docs with Sphinx (e.g. run sphinx-build -b html . _build/html) and inspect the generated documentation for example.var and example.MyString.var.
bug_report.ExpectedBehavior: Documentation for module-level variables and class attributes should show their types resolved according to autodoc_type_aliases (forward-reference name 'String' should be resolved to example.MyString as specified in the mapping).
bug_report.ObservedBehavior: In the generated documentation, module variable example.var and class attribute example.MyString.var display the unresolved forward-reference name ('String') instead of the mapped type (example.MyString). Function/method signatures that already pass type_aliases are not affected.

"""
    sphinx.builders.singlehtml
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

    Single HTML builders.

    :copyright: Copyright 2007-2020 by the Sphinx team, see AUTHORS.
    :license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.
"""

from os import path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Tuple, Union

from docutils import nodes
from docutils.nodes import Node

from sphinx.application import Sphinx
from sphinx.builders.html import StandaloneHTMLBuilder
from sphinx.deprecation import RemovedInSphinx40Warning, deprecated_alias
from sphinx.environment.adapters.toctree import TocTree
from sphinx.locale import __
from sphinx.util import logging, progress_message
from sphinx.util.console import darkgreen  # type: ignore
from sphinx.util.nodes import inline_all_toctrees

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SingleFileHTMLBuilder(StandaloneHTMLBuilder):
    """
    A StandaloneHTMLBuilder subclass that puts the whole document tree on one
    HTML page.
    """
    name = 'singlehtml'
    epilog = __('The HTML page is in %(outdir)s.')

    copysource = False

    def get_outdated_docs(self) -> Union[str, List[str]]:  # type: ignore
        return 'all documents'

    def get_target_uri(self, docname: str, typ: str = None) -> str:
        if docname in self.env.all_docs:
            # all references are on the same page...
            return self.config.master_doc + self.out_suffix + \
                '#document-' + docname
        else:
            # chances are this is a html_additional_page
            return docname + self.out_suffix

    def get_relative_uri(self, from_: str, to: str, typ: str = None) -> str:
        # ignore source
        return self.get_target_uri(to, typ)

    def fix_refuris(self, tree: Node) -> None:
        # fix refuris with double anchor
        fname = self.config.master_doc + self.out_suffix
        for refnode in tree.traverse(nodes.reference):
            if 'refuri' not in refnode:
                continue
            refuri = refnode['refuri']
            hashindex = refuri.find('#')
            if hashindex < 0:
                continue
            hashindex = refuri.find('#', hashindex + 1)
            if hashindex >= 0:
                refnode['refuri'] = fname + refuri[hashindex:]

    def _get_local_toctree(self, docname: str, collapse: bool = True, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        if 'includehidden' not in kwargs:
            kwargs['includehidden'] = False
        toctree = TocTree(self.env).get_toctree_for(docname, self, collapse, **kwargs)
        if toctree is not None:
            self.fix_refuris(toctree)
        return self.render_partial(toctree)['fragment']

        master = self.config.master_doc

        tree['docname'] = master
        self.env.resolve_references(tree, master, self)
        self.fix_refuris(tree)
        return tree

    def assemble_toc_secnumbers(self) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Tuple[int, ...]]]:
        # Assemble toc_secnumbers to resolve section numbers on SingleHTML.
        # Merge all secnumbers to single secnumber.
        #
        # Note: current Sphinx has refid confliction in singlehtml mode.
        #       To avoid the problem, it replaces key of secnumbers to
        #       tuple of docname and refid.
        #
        #       There are related codes in inline_all_toctres() and
        #       HTMLTranslter#add_secnumber().
        new_secnumbers = {}  # type: Dict[str, Tuple[int, ...]]
        for docname, secnums in self.env.toc_secnumbers.items():
            for id, secnum in secnums.items():
                alias = "%s/%s" % (docname, id)
                new_secnumbers[alias] = secnum

        return {self.config.master_doc: new_secnumbers}

    def assemble_toc_fignumbers(self) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Dict[str, Tuple[int, ...]]]]:
        # Assemble toc_fignumbers to resolve figure numbers on SingleHTML.
        # Merge all fignumbers to single fignumber.
        #
        # Note: current Sphinx has refid confliction in singlehtml mode.
        #       To avoid the problem, it replaces key of secnumbers to
        #       tuple of docname and refid.
        #
        #       There are related codes in inline_all_toctres() and
        #       HTMLTranslter#add_fignumber().
        new_fignumbers = {}  # type: Dict[str, Dict[str, Tuple[int, ...]]]
        # {'foo': {'figure': {'id2': (2,), 'id1': (1,)}}, 'bar': {'figure': {'id1': (3,)}}}
        for docname, fignumlist in self.env.toc_fignumbers.items():
            for figtype, fignums in fignumlist.items():
                alias = "%s/%s" % (docname, figtype)
                new_fignumbers.setdefault(alias, {})
                for id, fignum in fignums.items():
                    new_fignumbers[alias][id] = fignum

        return {self.config.master_doc: new_fignumbers}

    def get_doc_context(self, docname: str, body: str, metatags: str) -> Dict:
        # no relation links...
        toctree = TocTree(self.env).get_toctree_for(self.config.master_doc, self, False)
        # if there is no toctree, toc is None
        if toctree:
            self.fix_refuris(toctree)
            toc = self.render_partial(toctree)['fragment']
            display_toc = True
        else:
            toc = ''
            display_toc = False
        return {
            'parents': [],
            'prev': None,
            'next': None,
            'docstitle': None,
            'title': self.config.html_title,
            'meta': None,
            'body': body,
            'metatags': metatags,
            'rellinks': [],
            'sourcename': '',
            'toc': toc,
            'display_toc': display_toc,
        }

    def write(self, *ignored: Any) -> None:
        docnames = self.env.all_docs

        with progress_message(__('preparing documents')):
            self.prepare_writing(docnames)  # type: ignore

        with progress_message(__('assembling single document')):
            doctree = self.assemble_doctree()
            self.env.toc_secnumbers = self.assemble_toc_secnumbers()
            self.env.toc_fignumbers = self.assemble_toc_fignumbers()

        with progress_message(__('writing')):
            self.write_doc_serialized(self.config.master_doc, doctree)
            self.write_doc(self.config.master_doc, doctree)

    def finish(self) -> None:
        self.write_additional_files()
        self.copy_image_files()
        self.copy_download_files()
        self.copy_static_files()
        self.copy_extra_files()
        self.write_buildinfo()
        self.dump_inventory()

    @progress_message(__('writing additional files'))
    def write_additional_files(self) -> None:
        # no indices or search pages are supported

        # additional pages from conf.py
        for pagename, template in self.config.html_additional_pages.items():
            logger.info(' ' + pagename, nonl=True)
            self.handle_page(pagename, {}, template)

        if self.config.html_use_opensearch:
            logger.info(' opensearch', nonl=True)
            fn = path.join(self.outdir, '_static', 'opensearch.xml')
            self.handle_page('opensearch', {}, 'opensearch.xml', outfilename=fn)

class LaTeXBuilder(Builder):
    """
    Builds LaTeX output to create PDF.
    """
    name = 'latex'
    format = 'latex'
    epilog = __('The LaTeX files are in %(outdir)s.')
    if os.name == 'posix':
        epilog += __("\nRun 'make' in that directory to run these through "
                     "(pdf)latex\n"
                     "(use `make latexpdf' here to do that automatically).")

    supported_image_types = ['application/pdf', 'image/png', 'image/jpeg']
    supported_remote_images = False
    default_translator_class = LaTeXTranslator

    def init(self) -> None:
        self.babel = None           # type: ExtBabel
        self.context = {}           # type: Dict[str, Any]
        self.docnames = []          # type: Iterable[str]
        self.document_data = []     # type: List[Tuple[str, str, str, str, str, bool]]

        self.init_babel()
        self.init_multilingual()

    def get_outdated_docs(self) -> Union[str, List[str]]:

    def get_target_uri(self, docname: str, typ: str = None) -> str:
        if docname not in self.docnames:
            raise NoUri(docname, typ)
        else:
            return '%' + docname

    def get_relative_uri(self, from_: str, to: str, typ: str = None) -> str:
        # ignore source path
        return self.get_target_uri(to, typ)

    def init_document_data(self) -> None:
        preliminary_document_data = [list(x) for x in self.config.latex_documents]
        if not preliminary_document_data:
            logger.warning(__('no "latex_documents" config value found; no documents '
                              'will be written'))
            return
        # assign subdirs to titles
        self.titles = []  # type: List[Tuple[str, str]]
        for entry in preliminary_document_data:
            docname = entry[0]
            if docname not in self.env.all_docs:
                logger.warning(__('"latex_documents" config value references unknown '
                                  'document %s'), docname)
                continue

            if docname.endswith(SEP + 'index'):
                docname = docname[:-5]
            self.titles.append((docname, entry[2]))

    def init_context(self) -> None:
        self.context = DEFAULT_SETTINGS.copy()

        # Add special settings for latex_engine
        self.context.update(ADDITIONAL_SETTINGS.get(self.config.latex_engine, {}))

        # Add special settings for (latex_engine, language_code)
        if self.config.language:
            key = (self.config.latex_engine, self.config.language[:2])
            self.context.update(ADDITIONAL_SETTINGS.get(key, {}))

        # Apply user settings to context
        self.context.update(self.config.latex_elements)
        self.context['release'] = self.config.release
        self.context['use_xindy'] = self.config.latex_use_xindy

        if self.config.today:
            self.context['date'] = self.config.today
        else:
            self.context['date'] = format_date(self.config.today_fmt or _('%b %d, %Y'),
                                               language=self.config.language)

        if self.config.latex_logo:
            self.context['logofilename'] = path.basename(self.config.latex_logo)

        # for compatibilities
        self.context['indexname'] = _('Index')
        if self.config.release:
            # Show the release label only if release value exists
            self.context.setdefault('releasename', _('Release'))

    def update_context(self) -> None:
        """Update template variables for .tex file just before writing."""
        # Apply extension settings to context
        registry = self.app.registry
        self.context['packages'] = registry.latex_packages
        self.context['packages_after_hyperref'] = registry.latex_packages_after_hyperref

    def init_babel(self) -> None:
        self.babel = ExtBabel(self.config.language, not self.context['babel'])
        if self.config.language and not self.babel.is_supported_language():
            # emit warning if specified language is invalid
            # (only emitting, nothing changed to processing)

                           self.config.language)

    def init_multilingual(self) -> None:

            if not self.babel.uses_cyrillic():
                if 'X2' in self.context['fontenc']:
                    self.context['substitutefont'] = '\\usepackage{substitutefont}'
                    self.context['textcyrillic'] = '\\usepackage[Xtwo]{sphinxcyrillic}'
                elif 'T2A' in self.context['fontenc']:
                    self.context['substitutefont'] = '\\usepackage{substitutefont}'
                    self.context['textcyrillic'] = '\\usepackage[TtwoA]{sphinxcyrillic}'
            if 'LGR' in self.context['fontenc']:
                self.context['substitutefont'] = '\\usepackage{substitutefont}'
            else:
                self.context['textgreek'] = ''

        # 'babel' key is public and user setting must be obeyed
        if self.context['babel']:
            self.context['classoptions'] += ',' + self.babel.get_language()
            # this branch is not taken for xelatex/lualatex if default settings
            self.context['multilingual'] = self.context['babel']
            if self.config.language:
                self.context['shorthandoff'] = SHORTHANDOFF

                # Times fonts don't work with Cyrillic languages
                if self.babel.uses_cyrillic() and 'fontpkg' not in self.config.latex_elements:
                    self.context['fontpkg'] = ''
        elif self.context['polyglossia']:
            self.context['classoptions'] += ',' + self.babel.get_language()
            options = self.babel.get_mainlanguage_options()
            if options:
                language = r'\setmainlanguage[%s]{%s}' % (options, self.babel.get_language())
            else:
                language = r'\setmainlanguage{%s}' % self.babel.get_language()

            self.context['multilingual'] = '%s\n%s' % (self.context['polyglossia'], language)

    def write_stylesheet(self) -> None:
        highlighter = highlighting.PygmentsBridge('latex', self.config.pygments_style)
        stylesheet = path.join(self.outdir, 'sphinxhighlight.sty')
        with open(stylesheet, 'w') as f:
            f.write('\\NeedsTeXFormat{LaTeX2e}[1995/12/01]\n')
            f.write('\\ProvidesPackage{sphinxhighlight}'
                    '[2016/05/29 stylesheet for highlighting with pygments]\n\n')
            f.write(highlighter.get_stylesheet())

    def write(self, *ignored: Any) -> None:
        docwriter = LaTeXWriter(self)
        docsettings = OptionParser(
            defaults=self.env.settings,
            components=(docwriter,),
            read_config_files=True).get_default_values()  # type: Any
        patch_settings(docsettings)

        self.init_document_data()
        self.write_stylesheet()

        for entry in self.document_data:
            docname, targetname, title, author, themename = entry[:5]
            theme = self.themes.get(themename)
            toctree_only = False
            if len(entry) > 5:
                toctree_only = entry[5]
            destination = SphinxFileOutput(destination_path=path.join(self.outdir, targetname),
                                           encoding='utf-8', overwrite_if_changed=True)
            with progress_message(__("processing %s") % targetname):
                doctree = self.env.get_doctree(docname)
                toctree = next(iter(doctree.traverse(addnodes.toctree)), None)
                if toctree and toctree.get('maxdepth') > 0:
                    tocdepth = toctree.get('maxdepth')
                else:
                    tocdepth = None

                doctree = self.assemble_doctree(
                    docname, toctree_only,
                    appendices=(self.config.latex_appendices if theme.name != 'howto' else []))
                doctree['docclass'] = theme.docclass
                doctree['contentsname'] = self.get_contentsname(docname)
                doctree['tocdepth'] = tocdepth
                self.post_process_images(doctree)
                self.update_doc_context(title, author, theme)
                self.update_context()

            with progress_message(__("writing")):
                docsettings._author = author
                docsettings._title = title
                docsettings._contentsname = doctree['contentsname']
                docsettings._docname = docname
                docsettings._docclass = theme.name

                doctree.settings = docsettings
                docwriter.theme = theme
                docwriter.write(doctree, destination)

    def get_contentsname(self, indexfile: str) -> str:
        tree = self.env.get_doctree(indexfile)
        contentsname = None
        for toctree in tree.traverse(addnodes.toctree):
            if 'caption' in toctree:
                contentsname = toctree['caption']
                break

        return contentsname

    def update_doc_context(self, title: str, author: str, theme: Theme) -> None:
        self.context['title'] = title
        self.context['author'] = author
        self.context['docclass'] = theme.docclass
        self.context['papersize'] = theme.papersize
        self.context['pointsize'] = theme.pointsize
        self.context['wrapperclass'] = theme.wrapperclass

    def assemble_doctree(self, indexfile: str, toctree_only: bool, appendices: List[str]) -> nodes.document:  # NOQA
        self.docnames = set([indexfile] + appendices)
        logger.info(darkgreen(indexfile) + " ", nonl=True)
        tree = self.env.get_doctree(indexfile)
        tree['docname'] = indexfile
        if toctree_only:
            # extract toctree nodes from the tree and put them in a
            # fresh document
            new_tree = new_document('<latex output>')
            new_sect = nodes.section()
            new_sect += nodes.title('<Set title in conf.py>',
                                    '<Set title in conf.py>')
            new_tree += new_sect
            for node in tree.traverse(addnodes.toctree):
                new_sect += node
            tree = new_tree
        largetree = inline_all_toctrees(self, self.docnames, indexfile, tree,
                                        darkgreen, [indexfile])
        largetree['docname'] = indexfile
        for docname in appendices:
            appendix = self.env.get_doctree(docname)
            appendix['docname'] = docname
            largetree.append(appendix)
        logger.info('')
        logger.info(__("resolving references..."))
        self.env.resolve_references(largetree, indexfile, self)
        # resolve :ref:s to distant tex files -- we can't add a cross-reference,
        # but append the document name
        for pendingnode in largetree.traverse(addnodes.pending_xref):
            docname = pendingnode['refdocname']
            sectname = pendingnode['refsectname']
            newnodes = [nodes.emphasis(sectname, sectname)]  # type: List[Node]
            for subdir, title in self.titles:
                if docname.startswith(subdir):
                    newnodes.append(nodes.Text(_(' (in '), _(' (in ')))
                    newnodes.append(nodes.emphasis(title, title))
                    newnodes.append(nodes.Text(')', ')'))
                    break
            else:
                pass
            pendingnode.replace_self(newnodes)
        return largetree

    def apply_transforms(self, doctree: nodes.document) -> None:
        warnings.warn('LaTeXBuilder.apply_transforms() is deprecated.',
                      RemovedInSphinx40Warning, stacklevel=2)

    def finish(self) -> None:
        self.copy_image_files()
        self.write_message_catalog()
        self.copy_support_files()

        if self.config.latex_additional_files:
            self.copy_latex_additional_files()

    @progress_message(__('copying TeX support files'))
    def copy_support_files(self) -> None:
        """copy TeX support files from texinputs."""
        # configure usage of xindy (impacts Makefile and latexmkrc)
        # FIXME: convert this rather to a confval with suitable default
        #        according to language ? but would require extra documentation
        if self.config.language:
            xindy_lang_option = \
                XINDY_LANG_OPTIONS.get(self.config.language[:2],
                                       '-L general -C utf8 ')
            xindy_cyrillic = self.config.language[:2] in XINDY_CYRILLIC_SCRIPTS
        else:
            xindy_lang_option = '-L english -C utf8 '
            xindy_cyrillic = False
        context = {
            'latex_engine':      self.config.latex_engine,
            'xindy_use':         self.config.latex_use_xindy,
            'xindy_lang_option': xindy_lang_option,
            'xindy_cyrillic':    xindy_cyrillic,
        }
        logger.info(bold(__('copying TeX support files...')))
        staticdirname = path.join(package_dir, 'texinputs')
        for filename in os.listdir(staticdirname):
            if not filename.startswith('.'):
                copy_asset_file(path.join(staticdirname, filename),
                                self.outdir, context=context)

        # use pre-1.6.x Makefile for make latexpdf on Windows
        if os.name == 'nt':
            staticdirname = path.join(package_dir, 'texinputs_win')
            copy_asset_file(path.join(staticdirname, 'Makefile_t'),
                            self.outdir, context=context)

    @progress_message(__('copying additional files'))
    def copy_latex_additional_files(self) -> None:
        for filename in self.config.latex_additional_files:
            logger.info(' ' + filename, nonl=True)
            copy_asset_file(path.join(self.confdir, filename), self.outdir)

    def copy_image_files(self) -> None:
        if self.images:
            stringify_func = ImageAdapter(self.app.env).get_original_image_uri
            for src in status_iterator(self.images, __('copying images... '), "brown",
                                       len(self.images), self.app.verbosity,
                                       stringify_func=stringify_func):
                dest = self.images[src]
                try:
                    copy_asset_file(path.join(self.srcdir, src),
                                    path.join(self.outdir, dest))
                except Exception as err:
                    logger.warning(__('cannot copy image file %r: %s'),
                                   path.join(self.srcdir, src), err)
        if self.config.latex_logo:
            if not path.isfile(path.join(self.confdir, self.config.latex_logo)):
                raise SphinxError(__('logo file %r does not exist') % self.config.latex_logo)
            else:
                copy_asset_file(path.join(self.confdir, self.config.latex_logo), self.outdir)

    def write_message_catalog(self) -> None:
        formats = self.config.numfig_format
        context = {
            'addtocaptions': r'\@iden',
            'figurename': formats.get('figure', '').split('%s', 1),
            'tablename': formats.get('table', '').split('%s', 1),
            'literalblockname': formats.get('code-block', '').split('%s', 1)
        }

        if self.context['babel'] or self.context['polyglossia']:
            context['addtocaptions'] = r'\addto\captions%s' % self.babel.get_language()

        filename = path.join(package_dir, 'templates', 'latex', 'sphinxmessages.sty_t')
        copy_asset_file(filename, self.outdir, context=context, renderer=LaTeXRenderer())

    @property
    def usepackages(self) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
        warnings.warn('LaTeXBuilder.usepackages is deprecated.',
                      RemovedInSphinx50Warning, stacklevel=2)
        return self.app.registry.latex_packages

    @property

2. Opened sphinx/domains/std.py and inspected StandardDomain.get_fignumber, observing it indexes env.toc_secnumbers and env.toc_fignumbers by per-doc keys like env.toc_fignumbers[docname][figtype][id].
3. Opened sphinx/domains/math.py and inspected MathDomain.resolve_xref, observing it expects env.toc_fignumbers[docname]['displaymath'].get(node_id, ()) and therefore treats numbers as missing if docname is not a top-level key.
4. Opened sphinx/builders/singlehtml.py and inspected SingleFileHTMLBuilder.assemble_toc_fignumbers, observing it assembles a different mapping shape by encoding alias keys of the form "%s/%s" % (docname, figtype) and returning the mapping under the master_doc key.
5. Opened sphinx/environment/collectors/toctree.py and inspected TocTreeCollector.assign_figure_numbers, confirming that in normal (multi-page) builds the collector populates env.toc_fignumbers as a per-doc mapping env.toc_fignumbers[docname][figtype][id].
6. Identified root cause: a shape mismatch between single-file/aggregated builders (which assemble fignumbers/secnumbers under master_doc with "docname/figtype" alias keys) and consumers (std.MathDomain) that directly index by per-doc docname, causing lookups to miss numbers and trigger the "no number is assigned for ..." warnings.

Synthesis: The agent traced the warnings to a structural mismatch: single-file builders assemble numbering into a master_doc-scoped map keyed by "docname/figtype" aliases while StandardDomain and MathDomain assume per-doc mappings keyed by docname. This explains why direct indexing (env.toc_fignumbers[docname] or env.toc_secnumbers[docname]) fails in aggregated builds and leads to the spurious "no number is assigned for ..." warnings.
trajectory_phases.repair:
Key steps:
1. Attempted to apply a git patch for sphinx/domains/math.py but fell back to editing the file programmatically; updated MathDomain.resolve_xref to first try env.toc_fignumbers[docname].get('displaymath', ...) and then fall back to checking env.toc_fignumbers[master_doc].get("%s/%s" % (docname, 'displaymath')).
2. Programmatically patched sphinx/domains/std.py's get_fignumber to try per-doc lookups first and, on failure, fall back to master_doc-assembled alias lookups for both section numbers (toc_secnumbers) and figure numbers (toc_fignumbers) using aliases like "%s/%s" % (docname, figtype) or "%s/%s" % (docname, '#anchor').
3. Ran a quick import check (python importlib) to validate syntax; import failed at runtime due to missing dependency 'docutils' but the patched files were written without syntax errors.
4. Created patch.txt with git diff containing diffs for sphinx/domains/std.py and sphinx/domains/math.py and verified its contents.
5. Submitted the final output by printing COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT and cat-ing patch.txt (prepared patch file containing only the two modified source files).

Synthesis: The repairs add robust fallback lookups in sphinx/domains/std.py and sphinx/domains/math.py so consumers consult the master_doc-assembled alias maps when per-doc keys are absent, preventing ValueError and the spurious "no number is assigned for ..." warnings during single-file/aggregated builds. Changes were written and packaged into a git diff (patch.txt); basic syntax validation succeeded locally, although full runtime validation of the fix could not be completed in this environment due to a missing runtime dependency (docutils).

bug_report.Title: Sphinx 3.3: "no number is assigned for table:" warnings from mismatched env.toc_fignumbers shape in singlefile builds
bug_report.Description: After upgrading to Sphinx 3.3, building documentation with single-file/aggregated builders (singlehtml and similar aggregated doctree builds) produces repeated "WARNING: no number is assigned for table:" messages. The warnings are triggered when numref/figure/section/displaymath resolution fails to find assigned numbers during cross-reference resolution in single-file/aggregated builds.
bug_report.RootCause:
1. StandardDomain.get_fignumber (sphinx/domains/std.py::StandardDomain.get_fignumber) assumes env.toc_secnumbers and env.toc_fignumbers are keyed by each document's docname and looks up numbers as env.toc_secnumbers[docname] and env.toc_fignumbers[docname][figtype][id]. When the per-doc key is missing the method raises ValueError and its caller logs the "no number is assigned for ..." warning. (See std.py lines 1061-1071 and 1073-1079; warning logged in _resolve_numref_xref at lines 850-856.)

2. SingleFileHTMLBuilder.assemble_toc_fignumbers (sphinx/builders/singlehtml.py::SingleFileHTMLBuilder.assemble_toc_fignumbers) produces a different mapping shape for single-file builds: it encodes document+figtype into alias strings of the form "%s/%s" % (docname, figtype) and returns the mapping under a single top-level key (self.config.master_doc). Concretely, assemble_toc_fignumbers constructs new_fignumbers["docname/figtype"][id] = fignum and returns { master_doc: new_fignumbers } (singlehtml.py lines 116-123). This shape differs from the canonical per-doc mapping expected by consumers.

3. MathDomain.resolve_xref (sphinx/domains/math.py::MathDomain.resolve_xref) likewise indexes env.toc_fignumbers by docname and uses env.toc_fignumbers[docname]['displaymath'].get(node_id, ()) to obtain equation/displaymath numbers; if docname is not a top-level key it treats the numbers as missing (math.py lines 109-113).

4. TocTreeCollector.assign_figure_numbers (sphinx/environment/collectors/toctree.py::TocTreeCollector.assign_figure_numbers) is the canonical writer that populates the per-doc mapping env.toc_fignumbers[docname][figtype][id] in normal multi-page builds (toctree.py lines 218-259). The single-file alias mapping produced by SingleFileHTMLBuilder is therefore incompatible with consumers that only try the per-doc docname key.

5. The same aliasing pattern applies to section numbers in SingleFileHTMLBuilder.assemble_toc_secnumbers (singlehtml.py lines 96-102); StandardDomain.section number lookup similarly expects env.toc_secnumbers[docname] and can be affected by the same shape mismatch.

6. Repository-wide usage: direct indexing of env.toc_fignumbers by docname is present in sphinx/domains/std.py and sphinx/domains/math.py; other consumers (e.g., the regular HTML builder) use env.toc_fignumbers.get(docname, {}) safely (sphinx/builders/html/__init__.py line 591). The set of direct consumers found in the codebase aligns with the locations above.
StepsToReproduce: Upgrade a Sphinx project to version 3.3.

Build the documentation with the single-file HTML builder (singlehtml) or another build path that aggregates doctrees into a single document (the same aggregation approach is used by latex/aggregated-doctree paths).
Observe repeated warnings such as: "WARNING: no number is assigned for table:" and missing numbers in numref references in the generated output.
bug_report.ExpectedBehavior: Table/figure/section/displaymath references (numref/xref) are resolved and display their assigned numbers during single-file/aggregated builds; no spurious "no number is assigned for ..." warnings should appear when numbering was assigned by the environment collector.
bug_report.ObservedBehavior: Build output contains repeated warnings such as "WARNING: no number is assigned for table:" and other references (figures, sections, displaymath) appear without assigned numbers in single-file/aggregated builds, despite numbering being assigned in the environment collector.

# RE to strip backslash escapes
nl_escape_re = re.compile(r'\\\n')
strip_backslash_re = re.compile(r'\\(.)')


def optional_int(argument: str) -> int:
    """
    Check for an integer argument or None value; raise ``ValueError`` if not.
    """
    if argument is None:
        return None
    else:
        value = int(argument)
        if value < 0:
            raise ValueError('negative value; must be positive or zero')
        return value


class ObjectDescription(SphinxDirective):
    """
    Directive to describe a class, function or similar object.  Not used
    directly, but subclassed (in domain-specific directives) to add custom

    doc_field_types = []    # type: List[Field]
    domain = None           # type: str
    objtype = None          # type: str
    indexnode = None        # type: addnodes.index

    # Warning: this might be removed in future version. Don't touch this from extensions.
    _doc_field_type_map = {}  # type: Dict[str, Tuple[Field, bool]]

    def get_field_type_map(self) -> Dict[str, Tuple[Field, bool]]:
        if self._doc_field_type_map == {}:
            self._doc_field_type_map = {}
            for field in self.doc_field_types:
                for name in field.names:
                    self._doc_field_type_map[name] = (field, False)

                if field.is_typed:

        Retrieve the signatures to document from the directive arguments.  By
        default, signatures are given as arguments, one per line.
        """
        lines = nl_escape_re.sub('', self.arguments[0]).split('\n')
        if self.config.strip_signature_backslash:
            # remove backslashes to support (dummy) escapes; helps Vim highlighting
            return [strip_backslash_re.sub(r'\1', line.strip()) for line in lines]
        else:
            return [line.strip() for line in lines]

    def handle_signature(self, sig: str, signode: desc_signature) -> Any:
        """
        Parse the signature *sig* into individual nodes and append them to
        *signode*. If ValueError is raised, parsing is aborted and the whole
        *sig* is put into a single desc_name node.

        The return value should be a value that identifies the object.  It is
        passed to :meth:`add_target_and_index()` unchanged, and otherwise only
        used to skip duplicates.
        """
        raise ValueError

    def add_target_and_index(self, name: Any, sig: str, signode: desc_signature) -> None:
        """

        *name* is whatever :meth:`handle_signature()` returned.
        """
        return  # do nothing by default

        directive context on the build environment.
        """
        pass

        Called after creating the content through nested parsing,
        but before the ``object-description-transform`` event is emitted,
        and before the info-fields are transformed.
        Can be used to manipulate the content.
        """
        pass

    def after_content(self) -> None:
        """
        Called after parsing content. Used to reset information about the
        current directive context on the build environment.
        """
        pass

    def run(self) -> List[Node]:
        """
        Main directive entry function, called by docutils upon encountering the
        directive.

        This directive is meant to be quite easily subclassable, so it delegates
        to several additional methods.  What it does:

        * find out if called as a domain-specific directive, set self.domain
        * create a `desc` node to fit all description inside
        * parse standard options, currently `noindex`
        * create an index node if needed as self.indexnode
        * parse all given signatures (as returned by self.get_signatures())
          using self.handle_signature(), which should either return a name
          or raise ValueError
        * add index entries using self.add_target_and_index()
        * parse the content and handle doc fields in it
        """
        if ':' in self.name:
            self.domain, self.objtype = self.name.split(':', 1)
        else:
            self.domain, self.objtype = '', self.name
        self.indexnode = addnodes.index(entries=[])

        node = addnodes.desc()
        node.document = self.state.document
        node['domain'] = self.domain
        # 'desctype' is a backwards compatible attribute
        node['objtype'] = node['desctype'] = self.objtype
        node['noindex'] = noindex = ('noindex' in self.options)
        if self.domain:
            node['classes'].append(self.domain)

        self.names = []  # type: List[Any]
        signatures = self.get_signatures()
        for i, sig in enumerate(signatures):
            # add a signature node for each signature in the current unit
            # and add a reference target for it
            signode = addnodes.desc_signature(sig, '')
            self.set_source_info(signode)
            node.append(signode)
            try:
                # name can also be a tuple, e.g. (classname, objname);
                # this is strictly domain-specific (i.e. no assumptions may
                # be made in this base class)
                name = self.handle_signature(sig, signode)
            except ValueError:
                # signature parsing failed
                signode.clear()
                signode += addnodes.desc_name(sig, sig)
                continue  # we don't want an index entry here
            if name not in self.names:
                self.names.append(name)
                if not noindex:
                    # only add target and index entry if this is the first
                    # description of the object with this name in this desc block
                    self.add_target_and_index(name, sig, signode)

        contentnode = addnodes.desc_content()
        node.append(contentnode)
        if self.names:
            # needed for association of version{added,changed} directives
            self.env.temp_data['object'] = self.names[0]
        self.before_content()
        self.state.nested_parse(self.content, self.content_offset, contentnode)
        self.transform_content(contentnode)
        self.env.app.emit('object-description-transform',
                          self.domain, self.objtype, contentnode)
        DocFieldTransformer(self).transform_all(contentnode)
        self.env.temp_data['object'] = None
        self.after_content()
        return [self.indexnode, node]


class DefaultRole(SphinxDirective):
    """
    Set the default interpreted text role.  Overridden from docutils.
    """

    optional_arguments = 1
    final_argument_whitespace = False

    def run(self) -> List[Node]:
        if not self.arguments:
            docutils.unregister_role('')
            return []
        role_name = self.arguments[0]
        role, messages = roles.role(role_name, self.state_machine.language,
                                    self.lineno, self.state.reporter)
        if role:
            docutils.register_role('', role)
            self.env.temp_data['default_role'] = role_name
        else:
            literal_block = nodes.literal_block(self.block_text, self.block_text)
            reporter = self.state.reporter
            error = reporter.error('Unknown interpreted text role "%s".' % role_name,
                                   literal_block, line=self.lineno)
            messages += [error]

        return cast(List[nodes.Node], messages)


class DefaultDomain(SphinxDirective):
    """
    Directive to (re-)set the default domain for this source file.
    """

    has_content = False
    required_arguments = 1
    optional_arguments = 0
    final_argument_whitespace = False
    option_spec = {}  # type: Dict

    def run(self) -> List[Node]:
        domain_name = self.arguments[0].lower()
        # if domain_name not in env.domains:
        #     # try searching by label
        #     for domain in env.domains.values():
        #         if domain.label.lower() == domain_name:
        #             domain_name = domain.name
        #             break
        self.env.temp_data['default_domain'] = self.env.domains.get(domain_name)
        return []

def setup(app: "Sphinx") -> Dict[str, Any]:
    app.add_config_value("strip_signature_backslash", False, 'env')
    directives.register_directive('default-role', DefaultRole)
    directives.register_directive('default-domain', DefaultDomain)
    directives.register_directive('describe', ObjectDescription)
    # new, more consistent, name
    directives.register_directive('object', ObjectDescription)

    app.add_event('object-description-transform')

    return {
        'version': 'builtin',
        'parallel_read_safe': True,
        'parallel_write_safe': True,
    }

class PyField(PyXrefMixin, Field):
    def make_xref(self, rolename: str, domain: str, target: str,
                  innernode: "Type[TextlikeNode]" = nodes.emphasis,
                  contnode: Node = None, env: BuildEnvironment = None) -> Node:
        if rolename == 'class' and target == 'None':
            # None is not a type, so use obj role instead.
            rolename = 'obj'

        return super().make_xref(rolename, domain, target, innernode, contnode, env)


class PyGroupedField(PyXrefMixin, GroupedField):

    def make_xref(self, rolename: str, domain: str, target: str,
                  innernode: "Type[TextlikeNode]" = nodes.emphasis,
                  contnode: Node = None, env: BuildEnvironment = None) -> Node:
        if rolename == 'class' and target == 'None':
            # None is not a type, so use obj role instead.
            rolename = 'obj'

        return super().make_xref(rolename, domain, target, innernode, contnode, env)


class PyObject(ObjectDescription):
    """
    Description of a general Python object.

    :cvar allow_nesting: Class is an object that allows for nested namespaces
    :vartype allow_nesting: bool
    """
    option_spec = {
        'noindex': directives.flag,
        'noindexentry': directives.flag,
        'module': directives.unchanged,
        'annotation': directives.unchanged,
    }

    doc_field_types = [
        PyTypedField('parameter', label=_('Parameters'),
                     names=('param', 'parameter', 'arg', 'argument',
                            'keyword', 'kwarg', 'kwparam'),
                     typerolename='class', typenames=('paramtype', 'type'),
                     can_collapse=True),
        PyTypedField('variable', label=_('Variables'), rolename='obj',
                     names=('var', 'ivar', 'cvar'),
                     typerolename='class', typenames=('vartype',),
                     can_collapse=True),
        PyGroupedField('exceptions', label=_('Raises'), rolename='exc',
                       names=('raises', 'raise', 'exception', 'except'),
                       can_collapse=True),
        Field('returnvalue', label=_('Returns'), has_arg=False,
              names=('returns', 'return')),
        PyField('returntype', label=_('Return type'), has_arg=False,
                names=('rtype',), bodyrolename='class'),
    ]

    allow_nesting = False

    def get_signature_prefix(self, sig: str) -> str:
        """May return a prefix to put before the object name in the
        signature.
        """
        return ''

    def needs_arglist(self) -> bool:
        """May return true if an empty argument list is to be generated even if
        the document contains none.
        """
        return False

    def handle_signature(self, sig: str, signode: desc_signature) -> Tuple[str, str]:
        """Transform a Python signature into RST nodes.

        Return (fully qualified name of the thing, classname if any).

        If inside a class, the current class name is handled intelligently:
        * it is stripped from the displayed name if present
        * it is added to the full name (return value) if not present
        """
        m = py_sig_re.match(sig)
        if m is None:
            raise ValueError
        prefix, name, arglist, retann = m.groups()

        # determine module and class name (if applicable), as well as full name
        modname = self.options.get('module', self.env.ref_context.get('py:module'))
        classname = self.env.ref_context.get('py:class')
        if classname:
            add_module = False
            if prefix and (prefix == classname or
                           prefix.startswith(classname + ".")):
                fullname = prefix + name
                # class name is given again in the signature
                prefix = prefix[len(classname):].lstrip('.')
            elif prefix:
                # class name is given in the signature, but different
                # (shouldn't happen)
                fullname = classname + '.' + prefix + name
            else:
                # class name is not given in the signature
                fullname = classname + '.' + name
        else:
            add_module = True
            if prefix:
                classname = prefix.rstrip('.')
                fullname = prefix + name
            else:
                classname = ''
                fullname = name

3. Searched for the warning string "Malformed option description" across the repo (grep): found occurrences in locale/message files and confirmed that this is the user-facing warning emitted for option parsing failures.

Tokens like "[enable=]PATTERN" fail to match the restrictive regex (which disallows '[' and '=' in the name portion), and the handler currently logs a "Malformed option description" warning and skips such tokens, preventing them from being indexed or resolvable.

1. Attempted git apply with a prepared patch: the git apply attempt failed with "unrecognized input" and no patch was applied.
2. Programmatically modified sphinx/domains/std.py with a Python script: replaced the logic so that if option_desc_re.match(potential_option) succeeds it uses the regex groups, otherwise it treats the entire token as the option name (optname = potential_option) and sets args = ''. The change removes emitting the "Malformed option" warning for these cases.
3. Staged the change and inspected staged diff (git add && git diff --staged -- sphinx/domains/std.py): confirmed the diff shows the new permissive branch that accepts non-matching tokens as whole option names (preserving compatibility for tokens like "[enable=]PATTERN").
4. Attempted to run a small Python-based verification calling Cmdoption.handle_signature with desc_signature and the token "[enable=]PATTERN": the run failed with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'docutils', so runtime verification could not be completed in this environment.
5. Created patch.txt from the staged diff and submitted it (echo COMPLETE_TASK_AND_SUBMIT_FINAL_OUTPUT && cat patch.txt): produced the final patch file containing only changes to sphinx/domains/std.py and submitted it per the task instructions.  

Synthesis: The repair made the option parsing in Cmdoption.handle_signature permissive by accepting an entire token as the option name when the strict option_desc_re does not match, which preserves and indexes tokens like "[enable=]PATTERN" instead of emitting the "Malformed option description" warning and skipping them. The change was prepared, staged, and exported as a patch, but automated runtime verification could not be performed in this environment due to a missing docutils dependency; the patch file was created and submitted for integration and further testing.

bug_report.Title: Sphinx 3.2 rejects option:: tokens containing '[' or '=' (e.g. "[enable=]PATTERN")
bug_report.Description: When building documentation with Sphinx 3.2, tokens in the option:: directive that include characters like '[' or '=' (for example "[enable=]PATTERN") fail to be parsed by the standard domain. Sphinx emits a "Malformed option description" warning for such tokens and they are not indexed or resolvable; earlier Sphinx versions accepted these tokens without complaint.
bug_report.RootCause:
1) The option parser's regular expression is overly restrictive (location: sphinx/domains/std.py). option_desc_re is defined as:
   option_desc_re = re.compile(r'((?:/|--|-|\+)?[^\s=[]+)(=?\s*.*)')
   The first capture group [^\s=[]+ explicitly forbids '[' and '=' inside the option-name portion, so tokens containing those characters (for example "[enable=]PATTERN") do not match the regex and therefore are not recognized as valid option names.

2) Cmdoption.handle_signature treats any token that does not match option_desc_re as malformed and skips it (location: sphinx/domains/std.py, class Cmdoption, method handle_signature). The code calls option_desc_re.match(potential_option) and, if no match, logs the warning "Malformed option description %r, should look like ..." and continues without adding the token to signode['allnames']. This encodes the assumption that a non-match is necessarily a malformed option and must not be indexed.

3) If every token in a directive's signature fails to match, handle_signature raises ValueError (location: sphinx/domains/std.py, Cmdoption.handle_signature, lines after the token loop). This is a direct consequence of the skip-all-nonmatches behavior and can escalate the build failure beyond the emitted warnings.

4) The warning text itself is prescriptive about a narrow set of accepted forms (location: sphinx/domains/std.py, Cmdoption.handle_signature). The literal warning string lists only forms like "opt", "-opt args", "--opt args", "/opt args" and "+opt args", revealing the restricted expectation coded into the parser and user-facing message.

5) Downstream indexing and resolution rely on the canonical names produced by handle_signature (locations: Cmdoption.add_target_and_index; StandardDomain.add_program_option; StandardDomain._resolve_option_xref in sphinx/domains/std.py). Because non-matching tokens are not added to signode['allnames'], add_program_option is never called for them and they are not stored in StandardDomain.progoptions, so option cross-references and program-option indexing cannot resolve those tokens.

6) Compatibility impact / canonical-key considerations: the canonical key stored for a program option is exactly the optname produced by the regex parsing (optname = m.groups()[0]). Changes in what the parser accepts or how it splits name vs args will change those canonical keys. Tokens that were previously accepted but are now rejected had no stored key; accepting them later (or changing parsing) may change what keys appear in progoptions. This is a compatibility consideration for indexing and resolution (locations: same as item 5 and get_objects which yields progoptions entries).
StepsToReproduce:
Create an rst file containing an option directive using a token with '[' and '='; for example:

.. option:: [enable=]PATTERN

   Immediately enable events matching PATTERN
Run Sphinx 3.2 to build the documentation (for example: sphinx-build -b html sourcedir builddir).

Observe the build log: a warning like "Malformed option description '[enable=]PATTERN', should look like ..." appears (and may be treated as an error). The option is not indexed or resolvable in the generated output.
bug_report.ExpectedBehavior: Sphinx accepts program option syntax strings that are valid for the documented program (including tokens containing '[' or '=' where appropriate), does not emit a "Malformed option description" warning for those tokens, and renders/indexes the option so it is visible and resolvable in the generated documentation.
bug_report.ObservedBehavior: Sphinx 3.2 logs a "Malformed option description '[enable=]PATTERN' ..." warning for the option:: token and skips it. Because the token is not recorded in the standard domain's program-option index, the option is not resolvable or listed in produced indices. If all tokens in a signature are skipped, handle_signature raises ValueError, which can further cause the build to fail.

#: extended signature RE: with explicit module name separated by ::
py_ext_sig_re = re.compile(
    r'''^ ([\w.]+::)?            # explicit module name
          ([\w.]+\.)?            # module and/or class name(s)
          (\w+)  \s*             # thing name
          (?: \((.*)\)           # optional: arguments
           (?:\s* -> \s* (.*))?  #           return annotation
          )? $                   # and nothing more

def identity(x: Any) -> Any:
    return x


class _All:
    """A special value for :*-members: that matches to any member."""

    def __contains__(self, item: Any) -> bool:
        return True


class _Empty:
    """A special value for :exclude-members: that never matches to any member."""

    def __contains__(self, item: Any) -> bool:
        return False


ALL = _All()
EMPTY = _Empty()
UNINITIALIZED_ATTR = object()
INSTANCEATTR = object()
SLOTSATTR = object()


def members_option(arg: Any) -> Union[object, List[str]]:
    """Used to convert the :members: option to auto directives."""
    if arg in (None, True):
        return ALL
    elif arg is False:
        return None
    else:
        return [x.strip() for x in arg.split(',') if x.strip()]

    """Used to convert the :members: option to auto directives."""
    warnings.warn("members_set_option() is deprecated.",
                  RemovedInSphinx50Warning, stacklevel=2)
    if arg is None:
        return ALL
    return {x.strip() for x in arg.split(',') if x.strip()}


def exclude_members_option(arg: Any) -> Union[object, Set[str]]:
    """Used to convert the :exclude-members: option."""
    if arg in (None, True):
        return EMPTY
    return {x.strip() for x in arg.split(',') if x.strip()}


def inherited_members_option(arg: Any) -> Union[object, Set[str]]:
    """Used to convert the :members: option to auto directives."""
    if arg in (None, True):
        return 'object'
    else:
        return arg


def member_order_option(arg: Any) -> Optional[str]:
    """Used to convert the :members: option to auto directives."""
    if arg in (None, True):
        return None
    elif arg in ('alphabetical', 'bysource', 'groupwise'):
        return arg
    else:
        raise ValueError(__('invalid value for member-order option: %s') % arg)


SUPPRESS = object()


def annotation_option(arg: Any) -> Any:
    if arg in (None, True):
        # suppress showing the representation of the object
        return SUPPRESS
    else:
        return arg


def bool_option(arg: Any) -> bool:
    """Used to convert flag options to auto directives.  (Instead of
    directives.flag(), which returns None).
    """
    return True


def merge_special_members_option(options: Dict) -> None:
    """Merge :special-members: option to :members: option."""
    warnings.warn("merge_special_members_option() is deprecated.",
                  RemovedInSphinx50Warning, stacklevel=2)
    if 'special-members' in options and options['special-members'] is not ALL:
        if options.get('members') is ALL:
            pass
        elif options.get('members'):
            for member in options['special-members']:
                if member not in options['members']:
                    options['members'].append(member)
        else:
            options['members'] = options['special-members']


def merge_members_option(options: Dict) -> None:
    """Merge :*-members: option to the :members: option."""
    if options.get('members') is ALL:
        # merging is not needed when members: ALL
        return

    members = options.setdefault('members', [])
    for key in {'private-members', 'special-members'}:
        if key in options and options[key] not in (ALL, None):
            for member in options[key]:
                if member not in members:
                    members.append(member)


# Some useful event listener factories for autodoc-process-docstring.

def cut_lines(pre: int, post: int = 0, what: str = None) -> Callable:
    """Return a listener that removes the first *pre* and last *post*
    lines of every docstring.  If *what* is a sequence of strings,
    only docstrings of a type in *what* will be processed.

    Use like this (e.g. in the ``setup()`` function of :file:`conf.py`)::

       from sphinx.ext.autodoc import cut_lines
       app.connect('autodoc-process-docstring', cut_lines(4, what=['module']))

    This can (and should) be used in place of :confval:`automodule_skip_lines`.
    """
    def process(app: Sphinx, what_: str, name: str, obj: Any, options: Any, lines: List[str]
                ) -> None:
        if what and what_ not in what:
            return
        del lines[:pre]
        if post:
            # remove one trailing blank line.
            if lines and not lines[-1]:
                lines.pop(-1)
            del lines[-post:]
        # make sure there is a blank line at the end
        if lines and lines[-1]:
            lines.append('')
    return process


def between(marker: str, what: Sequence[str] = None, keepempty: bool = False,
            exclude: bool = False) -> Callable:
    """Return a listener that either keeps, or if *exclude* is True excludes,
    lines between lines that match the *marker* regular expression.  If no line
    matches, the resulting docstring would be empty, so no change will be made
    unless *keepempty* is true.

    If *what* is a sequence of strings, only docstrings of a type in *what* will
    be processed.
    """
    marker_re = re.compile(marker)

    def process(app: Sphinx, what_: str, name: str, obj: Any, options: Any, lines: List[str]
                ) -> None:
        if what and what_ not in what:
            return
        deleted = 0
        delete = not exclude
        orig_lines = lines[:]
        for i, line in enumerate(orig_lines):
            if delete:
                lines.pop(i - deleted)
                deleted += 1
            if marker_re.match(line):
                delete = not delete
                if delete:
                    lines.pop(i - deleted)
                    deleted += 1
        if not lines and not keepempty:
            lines[:] = orig_lines
        # make sure there is a blank line at the end
        if lines and lines[-1]:
            lines.append('')
    return process

class ObjectMember(tuple):

                 class_: Any = None, skipped: bool = False) -> None:

    def add_directive_header(self, sig: str) -> None:

        When it returns None value, autodoc-process-docstring will not be called for this
        object.

        if encoding is not None:
            warnings.warn("The 'encoding' argument to autodoc.%s.get_doc() is deprecated."
                          % self.__class__.__name__,
                          RemovedInSphinx40Warning, stacklevel=2)
        if ignore is not None:
            warnings.warn("The 'ignore' argument to autodoc.%s.get_doc() is deprecated."
                          % self.__class__.__name__,
                          RemovedInSphinx50Warning, stacklevel=2)
        docstring = getdoc(self.object, self.get_attr, self.config.autodoc_inherit_docstrings,

        if docstring:

            return [prepare_docstring(docstring, ignore, tab_width)]
